title: Export Compression
impact: 60-75% compression on large exports across all 7 formats

tasks.description: Add Compression Option to Export Types - Add ExportOptions interface with compress flag and quality, add ExportResult interface with compression metadata
acceptanceCriteria: ExportOptions interface with compress?: boolean and quality?: number

tasks.description: Implement Auto-Compression for Large Exports - Modify IOManager.exportGraph() to auto-compress when output >100KB, return ExportResult with compression metadata
acceptanceCriteria: Works with all 7 formats (JSON, CSV, GraphML, GEXF, DOT, Markdown, Mermaid)
Returns compression metadata (original size, compressed size, ratio)
Compression can be disabled via options

tasks.description: Update export_graph Tool Definition - Add optional compress parameter to export_graph tool schema with description and default behavior
acceptanceCriteria: compress parameter added to export_graph schema

tasks.description: Add Export Compression Tests - Test auto-compression threshold, all 7 formats, compression ratios, and metadata accuracy
Update Export Tool Documentation - Document compression behavior in tool definitions and update any export examples

notes: Can run in parallel with Sprint 2 and Sprint 4 after Sprint 1 completes. All 7 export formats benefit from compression, especially JSON and GraphML which have high redundancy.
